methods.description: Process for analyzing stable isotopes in planktonic foraminifera to determine δ 18 O values.

steps.description: 10 specimens of planktonic foraminifera G. sacculifer (without sac-like final chamber) were selected from the 315-400 µm size fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: select 10 specimens of G. sacculifer from 315-400 µm size fraction

steps.description: All isotope analyses were run on a Finnigan MAT 252 Mass Spectrometer with automated Kiel carbonate preparation device at IFM-GEOMAR (Kiel).
steps.searchable_summary: perform isotope analysis using Finnigan MAT 252 Mass Spectrometer

steps.description: The mass spectrometer was calibrated via the National Bureau of Standards (NBS) 19 and an internal laboratory standard to the Vienna Pee Dee Belemnite (PDB) scale.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate mass spectrometer to PDB scale

methods.description: Procedure for determining Element/Ca ratios in foraminiferal samples using ICP OES.

steps.description: Twenty to twenty-five specimens (∼550-800 µg) of G. ruber white, G. sacculifer (without sac-like final chamber), and N. dutertrei were selected from the 315-400 µm size fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: select 20-25 specimens from 315-400 µm size fraction

steps.description: Clay removal (water and methanol wash) and organic matter oxidation (hydrogen peroxide treatment) were applied to gently crushed samples.
steps.searchable_summary: remove clay and oxidize organic matter from samples

steps.description: Element/Ca ratios were performed on two inductively coupled plasma optical emission spectrometry (ICP OES) devices.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze Element/Ca ratios using ICP OES

methods.description: Quantification of mineral abundance in sediment samples using X-ray diffraction.

steps.description: Each sample was carefully grinded for five minutes in an agate mortar to homogenize the grain size to ∼5-10 µm.
steps.searchable_summary: grind samples to homogenize grain size

steps.description: The analysis was carried out on a Philips PW 1710 X-ray diffractometer containing a cobalt anode Kσ tube at 40 KV and 35 mA.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRD analysis using Philips PW 1710

steps.description: The peak-area method was deployed to quantify the aragonite content using the computer-based integration program MacDiff.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify aragonite content using peak-area method

methods.description: Assessment of diagenetic alteration in foraminiferal tests using Mg/Ca and Sr/Ca data.

steps.description: Visual evidence for diagenetic alteration of planktonic foraminiferal tests was combined with laser ablation results.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze diagenetic alteration using visual and laser ablation data

steps.description: A mass balance approach was applied to estimate the proportion of diagenetic overgrowth necessary to alter Mg/Ca and Sr/Ca.
steps.searchable_summary: apply mass balance model to estimate diagenetic overgrowth
